title: ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator wiring the loop eval to the HBR test-packet corpus
goal: Implement the Evaluator that the loop core (MT-148) consumes per Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET: validator first-pass PASS rate on the fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus. The evaluator runs the validator against each corpus item under a frozen sandbox (cluster B SandboxAdapter) and returns per-split PASS rates plus auxiliary metrics needed for the multi-metric promotion floor (latency p95, capsule bytes).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/evaluator.rs (ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator + EvalResult + SplitMetrics + ValidatorVerdict types). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/validator_first_pass_evaluator_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 (loop core) + MT-150 (corpus + split). Cluster B SandboxAdapter is consumed by trait (cross-cluster integration boundary).
implementation_notes: Per Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET operator decision: 'validator first-pass PASS rate on fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus'. Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS: 'multi-metric promotion floor (dev PASS up AND latency p95 not regressed AND capsule bytes not regressed AND holdout PASS rate not regressed)'. Surface: `pub struct ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator<'a> { sandbox: &'a dyn LoopSandbox, validator_runner: &'a dyn ValidatorRunner }` with `pub fn evaluate(split: &CorpusSplit, key_provider: &dyn KeyProvider, snapshot: &EditableSurfaceSnapshot) -> Result<EvalResult, EvalError>`. EvalResult: `{ train: SplitMetrics, dev: SplitMetrics, holdout: SplitMetrics, evaluated_at_utc, snapshot_hash: String }`. SplitMetrics: `{ pass_rate: f64 (0.0..=1.0), pass_count: u32, total_count: u32, latency_p95_ms: u64, capsule_bytes_p95: u64, per_item_results: Vec<PerItemResult> }`. PerItemResult: `{ item_id, verdict: ValidatorVerdict, latency_ms, capsule_bytes, error: Option<String> }`. Evaluation runs each corpus item through ValidatorRunner inside a sandboxed instance (so the editable surface snapshot is the ONLY change between baseline and trial runs); seed isolation per RW-6. Holdout evaluation decrypts via KeyProvider, runs items, re-encrypts holdout records before returning (holdout never leaves memory in plaintext form persistently). Tests: evaluator computes pass_rate correctly from per-item PASS/FAIL; latency p95 computed via standard order-statistic; capsule bytes p95 from injection records; holdout decryption fails cleanly without key; evaluator records snapshot_hash so EvalResult is traceable to its surface snapshot.
risk_if_missed: Without a typed evaluator the multi-metric promotion floor (MT-152) has nothing to gate against; the loop cannot measure improvement.
validator_focus: Evaluator is pure relative to sandbox + validator (both trait-injected). Per-split metrics computed independently. Holdout decryption is scoped to the eval call.

minimum_controls: Evaluator runs every item through the SandboxAdapter so editable surface changes are isolated from the host process.
Holdout decryption is scoped to a single eval call; plaintext is dropped after metrics are computed.
Latency and capsule-bytes p95 are computed from raw per-item observations, not from running averages.
